mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks including text generation, audio, and image processing without requiring GPU resources. Ideal for users seeking a decentralized and local-first AI solution.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for managing LocalAI deployments.

```bash

curl -Lo localai https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai

sudo mv localai /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, which is suitable for local development and testing.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Start Minikube with specified resources.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the Minikube cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> Set the current context to Minikube.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI to your Kubernetes cluster using the provided Helm chart.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI in the localai namespace.

steps.title: Verify LocalAI Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks if the LocalAI pods are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List all pods in the localai namespace to verify deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes LocalAI from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall LocalAI from the specified namespace.
> Delete the namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This step will upgrade LocalAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> Drain the LocalAI pods before upgrade.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the LocalAI pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n localai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Docker image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI application may be misconfigured or lacking necessary resources.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' and adjust resource limits if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server may be overloaded or unresponsive.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Kubernetes cluster with 'kubectl cluster-info' and ensure all nodes are healthy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The image specified in the deployment cannot be pulled from the registry.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and check network connectivity to the Docker registry.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved API endpoints for text generation.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older model formats; users should migrate to the new formats.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your model configurations to use the new formats as specified in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n localai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
